summaries.139143: A triager confirmed the issue on both stable and master on Android emulator. iOS still allows switching keyboards even with enableSuggestions: false, making behavior platform-inconsistent. One commenter noted this is correct behavior on iOS but not on Android. Currently stalled with no active assignee.
summaries.74300: Confirmed reproducible on stable and master channels. A commenter pointed to the layout code in input_decorator.dart where the label padding behavior changed, but no root cause was identified. The issue has been kept alive by community comments for several years with no fix or PR. Currently stalled.
summaries.119121: Confirmed reproducible on both iOS and Android in Flutter 3.7. The root cause identified by a triager is that the cursor position is not correctly translated into the loupe, causing the flashing. One community member expressed interest in contributing a fix but no PR was linked. Currently stalled.
summaries.53797: Root cause explained in comments: SelectableText registers a tap gesture internally to update the invisible cursor, which wins the GestureArena over the InkWell's tap behind it. A workaround using TapGestureRecognizer on TextSpan inside SelectableText.rich() is shared. The ink splash behavior is also affected. No fix or PR has been proposed.
summaries.145751: Confirmed reproducible on physical iPad devices (iPadOS 17.2+) but not on simulators. The bug only triggers after the magnifier appears during a long press, and it freezes silently with no console errors. A commenter linked related issues #142294, #138395, and #136244 suggesting a shared root cause where a native view blocks touch events after the magnifier is shown. Currently stalled with no active assignee.
summaries.137666: Confirmed reproducible on iOS 17.0.3 and 17.4. The root cause identified by @LongCatIsLooong is a UIKit bug: Flutter apps do not receive the `-[_UIKeyboardStateManager removeTextChoicePrompt]` call because it only fires for UIScene-based apps, and Flutter is not yet UIScene-based. Ongoing UIScene migration work (#167267) is expected to fix this. Currently waiting on the UIScene migration.
summaries.132270: A community member attempted a fix on a fork at https://github.com/rizumita/flutter/tree/spell-check. A commenter added platform-by-platform research showing three distinct behaviors across native apps: underline activates without focus, on focus, or only on typing. The original reporter noted iOS behavior is inconsistent even within the same flow. Currently stalled with no merged PR.
summaries.71342: Root cause identified in comments: when AlwaysScrollableScrollPhysics is set on the inner SelectableText's Scrollable, setCanDrag(true) is called, preventing the outer SingleChildScrollView from receiving scroll events. A workaround is to set physics on the outer scrollable widget. No fix or PR has been proposed. Currently stalled.
summaries.139201: Root cause identified by @LongCatIsLooong: the VerticalCaretMovementRun.isValid check fails because computeLineMetrics returns a different List instance each time when textAlign is not .left, making isValid always false. The bug also reproduces with regular TextField (not just Cupertino), confirmed via #165979. Still reproducible on master. Currently stalled with no active assignee.
summaries.141560: Confirmed reproducible on Android only (not iOS) on both stable and master. The bug only occurs with obscureText: true. Despite the text appearing to be inserted visually, the underlying text does not change (onChanged does not fire) and the character count remains at maxLength. No fix or PR has been proposed.
summaries.101222: Confirmed reproducible on web only (not macOS desktop) after removing focus from the field and refocusing in the middle of existing text. The wrong offset is used for the composing candidates box after refocus. No root cause or fix identified in comments. Currently stalled.
summaries.152391: Root cause identified in logs: the PDA keyboard (HT730) maps Shift Right's physical key to the Shift Left logical key, causing HardwareKeyboard._assertEventIsRegular to fail when a subsequent KeyUpEvent arrives with the correct Shift Right logical key. This is a device-specific key remapping issue on Android. No fix or PR has been proposed. Currently stalled.
summaries.125476: Confirmed reproducible on macOS desktop. The discussion explores using parentNode on Focus widgets to keep the TextField focused while toolbar buttons also receive focus, but @gspencergoog and @justinmc found that Shortcuts traversal always starts from the parent Focus, preventing key events from reaching the child. The technical approach of using an unfocusable bridge Focus widget with parentNode was proposed. Currently stalled with no PR.
summaries.178109: Regression introduced by #166804, confirmed broken from 3.35.0 onward and not present in 3.32.8. Affects Chrome and Firefox (not Safari) on web. Multiple users confirm the issue persists through 3.38.4 and 3.41.2. Currently stalled with no fix.
summaries.150460: This is a design proposal thread. @matthew-carroll provided an extensive critique arguing the current platform channel abstraction is a lowest-common-denominator approach that violates Flutter's own stated principles. @knopp discussed technical approaches for synchronous platform-thread/UI-thread communication via FFI. Discussion converged on the need for a declared mission to port all platform IME APIs verbatim, and a related issue #150525 was filed for the merged-thread approach.
summaries.106562:
Root cause identified: the bug is specific to text containing 

 (Windows-style double newlines), not 

. The cursor jumps to the beginning when clicking or arrowing onto the blank line between the 
 pairs, likely due to incorrect caret position calculation in TextPainter. Confirmed on Android and Linux. No fix or PR has been proposed.
summaries.139603: Confirmed reproducible on Windows web (Chrome) but not on Windows desktop or macOS Chrome. The unexpected delta behavior occurs when the app calls setEditingState to resync after the IME updates, causing a second delta insertion at the wrong offset. Currently stalled; assignee was removed due to no status updates.
summaries.121446: Confirmed reproducible only with Apple Handoff (universal clipboard from macOS to iOS physical device), not with local copy-paste. Related to #103163. The permission dialog flashes and closes before the user can respond on the first paste attempt; a second paste attempt works. iOS only. No fix or PR proposed; @hellohuanlin was CC'd.
summaries.101067: The bug is iPad-specific (not iPhone) and related to Scribble handwriting recognition. A commenter identified that commenting out engine-side Scribble lines in FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm fixes the issue. The bug worsens over time: after triggering, all widgets on the screen become unresponsive, the layout distorts, and the app requires a full relaunch. Still reproducible 3 years after filing with no fix.
summaries.147722: Root cause identified: the Netwrix Password Secure browser extension (available for Chrome and Firefox) interferes with Flutter web's text rendering, making the text transparent and the background white while editing. The issue does not reproduce with a plain HTML input element, indicating Flutter's DOM text input implementation is vulnerable to the extension's CSS injection. This is categorized as the same class of issue as #125125, to be addressed by the web text input redesign tracked in #139379.
summaries.135821: Confirmed reproducible on stable and master. A commenter identified that the size parameter in _ScaffoldLayout.performLayout is smaller than expected when Scaffold is placed inside a Column, but could not determine why. No fix or PR has been proposed. Currently stalled.
summaries.95920: The community provided a workaround using a Stack with Positioned icons instead of prefixIcon/suffixIcon, but noted the hardcoded contentPadding is incorrect. An improved workaround using suffixIcon: SizedBox.shrink() with Positioned is shared. No fix or PR has been proposed; the issue is treated as a proposal/feature request. Currently stalled.
summaries.141369: Confirmed reproducible on master channel. One commenter recently noted they could no longer reproduce it on the latest master, suggesting it may have been incidentally fixed. No explicit fix or PR was identified.
summaries.144936: Root cause: Flutter does not define shortcuts for Numpad navigation keys. PRs #145146 and #145464 were merged to add NumLock-aware shortcuts and numpad navigation for Linux. A Windows fix was prepared but blocked by #98377 (Windows NumLock state reporting bug). The issue is fixed on Linux, still broken on Windows. A side effect was reported where the Linux fix broke numpad number input in web apps on Linux browsers.
summaries.113376: Confirmed reproducible with both canvaskit and html renderers when viewing Flutter web inside a WebView or mobile browser. The cursor and selection handle positions are misaligned, with the handle appearing at the obscured-mode character positions even in non-obscured mode for password fields. Double selection handles are also observed. No root cause or fix was identified in comments. Currently stalled.
